title: Oliver Twist
author: Charles Dickens
description: A young orphan boy navigates the London criminal underworld after escaping a workhouse, exposing the harsh realities of poverty and injustice in Victorian England.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: An orphaned governess falls in love with her brooding employer Mr. Rochester, only to uncover dark secrets hidden within the walls of Thornfield Hall.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
description: A tale of obsessive love and revenge spanning two generations, centered on the tempestuous relationship between Heathcliff and Catherine on the Yorkshire moors.

title: Vanity Fair
author: William Makepeace Thackeray
description: The contrasting lives of two women — the ambitious Becky Sharp and the gentle Amelia Sedley — as they navigate love, money, and social climbing in Regency England.

title: David Copperfield
author: Charles Dickens
description: The semi-autobiographical story of a young man's journey from a troubled childhood through hardship and heartbreak to eventual success and self-discovery.

title: Bleak House
author: Charles Dickens
description: A sweeping critique of the British legal system told through the intertwined lives of characters caught in the endless lawsuit of Jarndyce and Jarndyce.

title: North and South
author: Elizabeth Gaskell
description: A young woman from the rural south of England is uprooted to an industrial northern town, where she confronts class conflict, labor disputes, and unexpected love.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
description: Set against the backdrop of the French Revolution, a story of love, sacrifice, and resurrection involving characters in London and Paris.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
description: An orphan named Pip receives a mysterious fortune and pursues his dream of becoming a gentleman, only to learn hard truths about wealth, loyalty, and identity.

title: Middlemarch
author: George Eliot
description: A richly detailed portrait of life in a fictional English Midlands town, following multiple characters as they navigate marriage, ambition, politics, and moral choices.

title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A respected London doctor creates a potion that unleashes his dark alter ego, exploring the duality of human nature and the dangers of unchecked ambition.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A beautiful young man sells his soul so that his portrait ages in his place, leading him down a path of moral corruption and eventual destruction.

title: Tess of the d'Urbervilles
author: Thomas Hardy
description: A young peasant woman's life is destroyed by the rigid moral codes of Victorian society after she is seduced and later seeks to reclaim her dignity.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A collection of twelve stories featuring the legendary detective Sherlock Holmes and his loyal companion Dr. Watson solving baffling crimes in Victorian London.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: Told through journals and letters, a Transylvanian vampire travels to England to spread the undead curse, and a determined group of friends band together to stop him.
